content:
# Production Engineering

**Summary**: The engineering of methods to maximize the recovery of hydrocarbons from a reservoir.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1993-kandil|Kandil, 1993]] applied production-engineering to optimize well placement and fracture design.

- [[2011-almubarak|ALMUBARAK, 2011]] applied production-engineering principles to optimize well performance.

- [[1999-fawumi|Fawumi, 1999]] focused on production-engineering to address condensate blockage issues.

## Key Subtopics

- [[1993-kandil|Kandil, 1993]] ['well design, completion strategy', 'fracture treatment']

- [[2011-almubarak|ALMUBARAK, 2011]] applied production-engineering principles to optimize well performance.

- [[1999-fawumi|Fawumi, 1999]] focused on production-engineering to address condensate blockage issues.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[1993-kandil|Kandil, 1993]] ['experimental modeling', 'simulation']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Horizontal wells with longitudinal fractures were found to be effective for increasing contact area in low permeability zones. ([[1993-kandil|Kandil, 1993, p. 76]])

- Optimized production rates from individual laterals by setting downhole valves at appropriate positions. ([[2011-almubarak|ALMUBARAK, 2011, p. 17]])

- A production rate of 40 MSCF/D was identified as a balance for maximizing recovery while managing condensate dropout. ([[1999-fawumi|Fawumi, 1999, p. 100]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[1993-kandil|Kandil, 1993]] Managing water breakthrough and fracture interference.
